accurate.why: All claims match the bill text, and contextual claims about the AECA Section 36(c) review/disapproval process and DCS exports are accurate.

no_hallucinations.why: Added context about the Arms Export Control Act disapproval mechanism is accurate and verifiable, not fabricated.

states_purpose.why: It clearly states the bill is a joint resolution to disapprove a specific proposed defense export to Israel.

changes_to_existing_law.why: The summary correctly identifies the resolution invokes the Arms Export Control Act's Section 36(c) disapproval authority to block the transaction.

exceptions_conditions.why: It notes the key limit that the resolution addresses only this single export and contains no other provisions or carve-outs.

effective_dates.why: The bill specifies no effective dates or sunsets, and the summary correctly notes the absence of stated effective dates.

major_provisions_covered.why: It captures the sole operative effect: prohibiting the export described in Transmittal DDTC 23-077, including the 20,000 rifles to the named entity.

objective_tone.why: The summary is neutral and descriptive with no advocacy or editorializing.

coherent.why: The summary is well-organized, logically structured, and internally consistent.

concise.why: The summary is appropriately brief with minimal padding while providing useful context.

correct_entities.why: Transmittal No. DDTC 23-077, Section 36(c) of the AECA, M.R.D. Efram Investments Ltd., and the Israel National Police are all correctly named per the bill.

correct_figures.why: The $1,000,000 threshold, 20,000 rifles, 11.5-inch barrel, 5.56mm caliber, and March 24, 2025 date all match the bill text.
